objectClass.notes: The image presents a stylized procedural reconstruction. Hidden port-side and lower-hull forms are inferred by bilateral structure and maritime construction logic.

reasoning: The long crescent hull, two-storey cabin, two roof tiers, mast, sail, and rigging create a heavily interrupted silhouette.
Twenty-four visible or symmetry-inferred oars plus shields, rail posts, shoji grids, roof ribs, and lanterns require deterministic repetition systems.
Oars, sail, lanterns, cabin, and roof tiers require stable pivots, sockets, colliders, and detachable groups.

specDepthDecision.rationale: Reference identity depends on repeated articulated parts and nested architectural masses, so a flat or merged representation is insufficient.

definitionOfDone: At the design camera the model must read as the pictured tower ship: a long upward-curving timber hull carrying a two-storey shoji cabin, layered dark roofs, a tall tan sail, shields, lanterns, railings, and a dense articulated oar bank.
Blockout, hierarchy, material, and interaction modes must expose the same semantic object factory without rebuilding unrelated scene infrastructure.
Every oar pivots from a named gunwale socket; cabin, roof, mast, sail, lanterns, and shield banks remain independently targetable.

featureGroups.name: Curved fortified hull and raised end posts
qualityCriteria: The hull spans roughly 20 world units, narrows toward both ends, rises at bow and stern, and retains a continuous upper gunwale.

featureGroups.name: Two-storey shoji cabin and layered pagoda roofs
qualityCriteria: Two white gridded wall tiers sit over a dark timber frame, each capped by an overhanging segmented roof with upturned corners.

featureGroups.name: Mast, segmented tan sail, and rigging system
qualityCriteria: The tall mast rises above the cabin, the sail occupies the upper-right silhouette, and at least four tension lines connect mast, hull, and sail corners.

featureGroups.name: Twenty-four articulated oars
qualityCriteria: Twelve oars per side are distributed along the hull, share consistent hinge sockets, and move from their roots without visible gaps.

featureGroups.name: Shields, lanterns, railings, stairs, and prow ornament
qualityCriteria: Secondary systems preserve the reference rhythm and remain subordinate to the four primary masses.

featureGroups.name: Aged timber, cloth, paper, and metal response
qualityCriteria: Independent material zones provide dark structural timber, warm planks, pale panels, tan cloth, and restrained metal highlights with nonuniform roughness.
failureModes: lighting-painted color treated as exact PBR

visualDeltaChecks: cabin tier spacing, roof overhang, and corner lift
mast/sail height and rigging attachment continuity
24-oar count, hinge alignment, and repeated spacing
warm wood versus pale panel versus tan sail material separation
antiShallowSpecRules: Reject a merged decorative mesh that removes oar, sail, cabin, roof, or lantern pivots.
Reject a hull generated from one box or capsule without station-based taper and end rise.
Reject material acceptance based on flat colors or exact-PBR claims from the single composite screenshot.
Reject any oar, rigging line, rail, lantern, or mast attachment with a visible air gap at its parent socket.

authoringInstruction: Use the assessment as the shared acceptance contract for the spec, demo factory, routes, and evidence capture.
